html:
AMD Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (~$353.66) HIGH RISK

⇗ Top

Price: ~$353.66 | Entry Zone: $280–300

Technicals

  • Price is in a steep, near-vertical parabolic move, significantly extended above historical trend structure.
  • RSI is in deep overbought territory, indicating extreme momentum that is prone to mean reversion.
  • The current price is far removed from the established diagonal trendline support, suggesting a lack of a solid technical base for current levels.
  • No recent buy signals; the last signal was a sell dot, and the oscillator lines are extended.

News & Financials

  • AMD continues to benefit from strong demand in the AI data center GPU market.
  • High valuation multiples currently priced in, leaving little room for error in upcoming earnings reports.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Wait for a pullback to the 280-300 range or a consolidation period that allows the RSI to cool off.
  • Risk: High risk of a sharp correction due to the parabolic nature of the recent price action and overextended technical indicators.
  • Action: Recommend trimming the position by 50% (~20.0 shares) to lock in gains and reduce exposure to a potential mean reversion, while holding the remaining 50% to capture potential further momentum.
action: Recommend trimming the position by 50% (~20.0 shares) to lock in gains and reduce exposure to a potential mean reversion, while holding the remaining 50% to capture potential further momentum.

html:
BE Bloom Energy Corporation (~$280.47) HIGH RISK

⇗ Top

Price: ~$280.47 | Entry Zone: $140–160

Technicals

  • Price is currently significantly extended above the established diagonal trendline channel, indicating a potential overextension.
  • RSI is in overbought territory (above 70), suggesting a high probability of a near-term pullback or consolidation.
  • The stock has experienced a parabolic vertical move, deviating from the previous trend structure.
  • Recent price action shows a large bearish candle, signaling potential exhaustion of the current rally.

News & Financials

  • Bloom Energy operates in the volatile clean energy sector, which is highly sensitive to interest rate expectations and government policy.
  • The company has historically faced challenges with profitability and cash flow, making the current valuation premium risky.
  • Market sentiment is currently driven by speculative interest in energy infrastructure for AI data centers.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Wait for a retest of the upper diagonal trendline or a consolidation back toward the 160-180 range.
  • Risk: High risk of a mean reversion as the price is currently trading far from its established trend channel and RSI is overextended.
  • Action: Trim position by 50% (~7.5 shares) to lock in gains from the recent parabolic move, and hold the remaining 7.5 shares to manage risk while maintaining exposure.
action: Trim position by 50% (~7.5 shares) to lock in gains from the recent parabolic move, and hold the remaining 7.5 shares to manage risk while maintaining exposure.

DGRO iShares Core Dividend Growth ETF (DGRO) (~$73.49) WAIT

⇗ Top

Price: ~$73.49 | Entry Zone: $70.00–72.00

Technicals

  • Price is currently near recent highs, showing signs of resistance.
  • RSI is at 70.21, indicating overbought conditions, which often precedes a pullback.
  • The purple and orange RSI lines are tightly converged at the top, suggesting weakening momentum.
  • The most recent 'Buy' signal occurred lower in the trend; current levels lack a fresh entry trigger.

News & Financials

  • DGRO focuses on companies with a history of growing dividends, providing defensive stability.
  • Market sentiment remains elevated, but dividend-focused ETFs often face pressure when interest rates remain higher for longer.
  • Current price is approaching the previous peak, making it a less attractive entry point for new capital.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Wait for a pullback toward the 70.00-71.00 support area or a bullish crossover on the RSI.
  • Risk: Moderate; the asset is currently overextended in the short term, increasing the risk of a mean reversion.
  • Action: Hold current position (97.0 shares). Do not add at these levels. If the RSI crosses downward, consider a trim position by 25% (~24.2 shares) to lock in gains.

IREN Iris Energy Limited (~$44.52) WAIT

⇗ Top

Price: ~$44.52 | Entry Zone: $32.00–36.00

Technicals

  • Price is currently testing the upper resistance of a descending triangle pattern, showing signs of rejection.
  • The RSI is neutral (48.43) with the purple line recently crossing below the orange line, indicating weakening momentum.
  • The most recent 'Sell' signal dot suggests potential short-term downside pressure.
  • The stock is trading below the major diagonal resistance line established from the previous peak.

News & Financials

  • IREN is highly sensitive to Bitcoin price volatility and broader crypto-mining sector sentiment.
  • As a growth-oriented mining company, it faces significant capital expenditure requirements and energy cost fluctuations.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Wait for a confirmed breakout above the descending trendline or a retest of the lower support zone near 32.00-36.00.
  • Risk: High volatility; failure to break the descending resistance could lead to a retest of the lower triangle support.
  • Action: Hold current position (1.1%) and wait for a clearer directional breakout; do not add until the trendline is decisively cleared or support is retested.

html:
LLY Eli Lilly and Company (~$939.71) WAIT

⇗ Top

Price: ~$939.71 | Entry Zone: $875–900 or above 975

Technicals

  • The stock is currently in a downtrend, indicated by the yellow diagonal trendline from the late February peak.
  • Price has recently bounced strongly from lows around $850-$875, but is approaching the overhead downtrend resistance (approx. $950-$975).
  • The RSI (purple line at 58.76) is significantly above its signal line (orange line at 41.86) and both are trending upwards, indicating strong bullish momentum in the short term.
  • A 'Buy' signal dot appeared around the $875-$900 price level in late April/early May.
  • Previous 'Sell' signal dots were observed around $950-$975 (mid-April) and $1050 (late March).

News & Financials

  • Financials and news are not visible in the provided chart.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Wait for a confirmed breakout above the downtrend line (approx. $975-$1000) or a pullback to the recent support level (approx. $875-$900).
  • Risk: Medium to High. The stock is in a downtrend and currently testing overhead resistance. While RSI shows bullish momentum, a failure to break out could lead to further downside. A small position reduces overall portfolio risk.
  • Action: Hold current small position (1.2%, ~5.0 shares). Do not add at current levels due to overhead resistance. Wait for a clear breakout above the downtrend line or a pullback to a more established support level before considering adding to the position.

SLV iShares Silver Trust (~$66.77) WAIT

⇗ Top

Price: ~$66.77 | Entry Zone: $60.00–64.00

Technicals

  • Price is currently trading between a major long-term support trendline and a shorter-term broken uptrend line.
  • RSI is neutral at 47.15, showing no clear momentum direction.
  • Recent price action shows a 'Sell' signal dot, suggesting potential overhead resistance.
  • The purple/orange RSI lines are currently intertwined, indicating a lack of a strong trend signal.

News & Financials

  • SLV tracks the spot price of silver, which is highly sensitive to macroeconomic factors like interest rates and industrial demand.
  • Silver has recently pulled back from highs above $110, reflecting volatility in precious metals markets.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Look for a retest of the lower long-term diagonal support trendline near $60-$62 or a bullish crossover of the RSI lines.
  • Risk: Moderate; the asset is in a corrective phase after a significant parabolic move, and breaking below the long-term trendline would be bearish.
  • Action: Hold current position (150.0 shares). The current allocation is slightly elevated, but the price is not yet at a clear buy or sell trigger point based on the provided indicators.

html:
SOFI SoFi Technologies, Inc. (~$16.08) NEAR ZONE

⇗ Top

Price: ~$16.08 | Entry Zone: $15.00–16.50

Technicals

  • Price is currently testing a long-term upward sloping trendline support.
  • The RSI shows a recent bullish crossover with the purple line crossing above the orange line, suggesting potential momentum shift.
  • A 'Sell' signal dot appeared recently, indicating short-term overhead resistance.
  • The stock is trading below the previous major downward trendline, indicating a broader bearish structure that needs to be reclaimed.

News & Financials

  • SoFi continues to navigate a high-interest-rate environment which impacts lending margins.
  • Market sentiment remains sensitive to earnings reports and guidance on loan origination volume.
  • The company is focused on achieving GAAP profitability, which is a key catalyst for long-term valuation.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Accumulation near the 15.50-16.00 support trendline is favorable if it holds.
  • Risk: High volatility; failure to hold the current trendline support could lead to a retest of lower support levels.
  • Action: Hold current position. Given the 1.9% allocation is near the 2% target, wait for a confirmed breakout above the recent 'Sell' signal high before adding more. If the trendline support breaks decisively, consider a reduction of 25% (~112.5 shares) to manage risk.

STX Seagate Technology Holdings plc (STX) (~$652.85) HIGH RISK

⇗ Top

Price: ~$652.85 | Entry Zone: $Not currently in an ideal entry zone; awaiting a significant pullback and consolidation.

Technicals

  • STX is in a strong, accelerated uptrend since late 2025, reaching new highs in May 2026.
  • A 'Sell' signal dot appeared near the recent peak around $680-$700.
  • The Relative Strength Index (RSI) is highly overbought, with the purple line at 82.74 and the orange signal line at 76.61.
  • The price has pulled back slightly from its recent high, but remains elevated.

News & Financials

  • No specific news or financial data is visible in the provided chart.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Not recommended to buy at current levels due to overbought conditions and recent sell signal. A healthier entry would involve a significant price correction and consolidation, potentially around previous support levels or after the RSI cools down.
  • Risk: High risk for new entry or adding to the position due to the extended rally and highly overbought RSI, indicating potential for a significant correction.
  • Action: Given the current 3.0% allocation (18.0 shares), the highly overbought RSI, and the recent 'Sell' signal dot, it is prudent to reduce exposure. Recommend trimming the position by 25% (~4.5 shares) to lock in some profits and reduce risk.

VEA Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets ETF (~$68.71) WAIT

⇗ Top

Price: ~$68.71 | Entry Zone: $64.00–66.00

Technicals

  • Price is currently testing recent highs, showing signs of potential resistance near $69.00.
  • RSI is at 57.01, indicating neutral momentum without a clear overbought or oversold signal.
  • The most recent 'Buy' signal occurred at a lower level, and the current price is extended from that entry point.
  • Trend structure remains bullish, but the price is currently consolidating after a sharp move up from the $64.00 support area.

News & Financials

  • VEA provides broad exposure to developed markets outside the U.S., making it sensitive to global economic growth and currency fluctuations.
  • As an ETF, it is generally less volatile than individual stocks, but it is currently trading near the upper end of its recent range.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Look for a pullback toward the $64.00-$66.00 range where the previous 'Buy' signal was generated.
  • Risk: Moderate; the current price is extended from the recent consolidation zone, increasing the risk of a short-term pullback.
  • Action: Hold current position (148.0 shares). The position is slightly overweight (2.6%) relative to a standard 1-2% starter, so avoid adding more until a clearer pullback or a breakout above $70.00 is confirmed.

VWO Vanguard Emerging Markets Stock Index Fund ETF (~$58.75) HIGH RISK

⇗ Top

Price: ~$58.75 | Entry Zone: $54.00–56.00

Technicals

  • Price is currently trading near the upper end of its recent range, showing signs of exhaustion.
  • RSI is trending downward after a recent peak, suggesting weakening momentum.
  • The most recent 'Buy' signal occurred at a lower price level, and the current price is extended from that entry.
  • The stock is approaching previous resistance levels where selling pressure has historically increased.

News & Financials

  • VWO is heavily influenced by geopolitical tensions and economic policies in emerging markets, particularly China.
  • Emerging markets often exhibit high volatility compared to developed markets, increasing risk at current elevated price levels.
  • The fund is sensitive to fluctuations in the US Dollar; a strong dollar typically acts as a headwind for emerging market assets.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Wait for a pullback toward the 54.00-56.00 support zone where the previous 'Buy' signals were generated.
  • Risk: High; the position is currently over-allocated at 10.5% and the price is extended, increasing the risk of a mean-reversion pullback.
  • Action: Recommend trimming the position by 25% (~175.0 shares) to lock in gains and reduce exposure to 7.8%, then hold the remaining 525.0 shares.

  • The most recent 'Buy' signal occurred at a lower price point, suggesting current levels are extended.
  • Trend structure remains bullish, but the lack of a recent pullback makes current entry unfavorable for risk-adjusted returns.

News & Financials

  • VXUS provides broad exposure to international markets, which are currently sensitive to global interest rate policies and currency fluctuations.
